reasoning: This kernel does essentially no arithmetic—just one global load and one global store per element—so its arithmetic intensity is ~0 FLOP/byte, far below the A10G ridge point of 62 FLOP/byte. The access pattern is still coalesced despite the fixed +32 element offset, so performance will be limited by DRAM bandwidth rather than compute, with high memory throughput expected and no signs of register pressure or low-occupancy issues from the simple kernel structure.
raw_response:

The access pattern is still coalesced despite the fixed +32 element offset, so performance will be limited by DRAM bandwidth rather than compute, with high memory throughput expected and no signs of register pressure or low-occupancy issues from the simple kernel structure.
